skill.name: academic-highlight-generator
skill.description: Generates submission-ready Elsevier/SCI Highlights from manuscript text or extracted PDF/DOCX/TXT content. Use when a user needs 3-5 concise, evidence-grounded highlight bullets for a research paper, review, meta-analysis, case report, or bioinformatics manuscript.
skill.summary: Generates submission-ready Elsevier/SCI Highlights from manuscript text or extracted PDF/DOCX/TXT content with automatic article type classification and self-critique refinement.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer flagged 41 potential issues across 4 files (695 lines) with a computed risk score of 100/100. After manual evaluation, all 41 findings are confirmed false positives. The 4 'weak cryptographic algorithm' findings matched innocuous text in YAML frontmatter, prompt templates, and a legacy JSON audit file — no cryptographic code exists in this skill. The 35 'backtick execution' findings are standard markdown inline code formatting in SKILL.md, not shell execution. The 2 'hardcoded URL' findings are a legitimate GitHub source attribution link. The skill instructs an AI to run a local Python text extraction script, which is documented as a risk factor but is appropriate for the skill's purpose. No prompt injection attempts, data exfiltration patterns, or malicious intent detected.

dangerous_patterns.title: Markdown backtick formatting flagged as shell execution (false positive)
dangerous_patterns.description: The static analyzer flagged 35 backtick characters in SKILL.md as Ruby/shell backtick execution. All instances are standard markdown inline code formatting (e.g., `Highlights`, `.docx`) or code block fences. SKILL.md is an AI instruction document, not an executable script. The two explicit python commands at lines 73-75 and 103-105 are legitimate skill workflow instructions for a local text extraction utility.

dangerous_patterns.title: Weak cryptographic algorithm flagged in non-code files (false positive)
dangerous_patterns.description: The static analyzer flagged 'weak cryptographic algorithm' at SKILL.md:3, SKILL.md:25, references/prompts.md:59, and academic-highlight-generator_audit_result_v1.json:62. These locations contain YAML frontmatter text, file format instructions, prompt template text, and legacy JSON audit data respectively. No cryptographic code exists in any skill file.

dangerous_patterns.title: Hardcoded GitHub URL flagged as network risk (false positive)
dangerous_patterns.description: The static analyzer flagged two hardcoded URL instances at SKILL.md:7. This is a legitimate GitHub source attribution link in markdown format (https://github.com/aipoch/medical-research-skills). It is not a data exfiltration endpoint or malicious network request.

content.user_title: Generate Journal-Ready Academic Highlights from Manuscripts
content.value_statement: Writing Elsevier/SCI Highlights for manuscript submission is tedious and error-prone. This skill automatically generates 3-5 concise, evidence-grounded highlight bullets from your manuscript text or files, saving hours of manual formatting.
actual_capabilities: Extract text from PDF, DOCX, and TXT manuscript files using a Python helper script
Classify manuscripts into 10 article types including original research, meta-analysis, and case reports
Generate 3-5 highlight bullets under 85 characters each with objective third-person tone
Self-critique and refine highlights against source material for accuracy and completeness
Refuse to generate highlights when source text is insufficient or article type is unsupported
Output a submission-ready Highlights section with proper formatting for journal submission systems
limitations: Cannot process binary .doc files; users must convert to .docx or .pdf first
Cannot fabricate missing results, novelty claims, or conclusions not present in the source text
Output quality depends on the completeness and clarity of the provided source material
Requires Python with pypdf and python-docx libraries installed for file extraction functionality

use_cases.title: Researcher Preparing Journal Submission
use_cases.description: A researcher needs to generate the required Highlights section for an Elsevier or SCI journal submission from their completed manuscript text or file.

use_cases.title: Graduate Student Converting Thesis to Articles
use_cases.description: A graduate student converting thesis chapters into journal articles needs properly formatted highlights for each submission to meet journal guidelines.

use_cases.title: Editorial Assistant Verifying Submission Compliance
use_cases.description: An editorial assistant handling multiple manuscript submissions needs to quickly generate or verify highlight sections for compliance with journal formatting requirements.

prompt_templates.title: Basic highlights generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate Highlights for my research paper. Here is the manuscript text: [paste manuscript]
prompt_templates.scenario: User has plain text and wants quick highlights without specifying article type or advanced options.

prompt_templates.title: File-based extraction and generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract text from this PDF and generate 3-5 Highlights bullets suitable for Elsevier submission: [file path]
prompt_templates.scenario: User has a manuscript file instead of pasted text and needs extraction before highlight generation.

prompt_templates.title: Type-specific generation for meta-analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate Highlights for this meta-analysis manuscript with emphasis on search strategy, number of studies, and pooled results: [paste text]
prompt_templates.scenario: User knows the article type and wants generation tailored to the specific conventions of meta-analysis highlights.

prompt_templates.title: Full workflow with classification and refinement
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate Highlights from this manuscript. Classify the article type, select the appropriate generation template, self-critique the output, and refine to meet the 85-character limit and objective tone requirements: [paste text]
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants the complete automated workflow including article classification, template selection, generation, critique, and refinement.

output_examples.input: A randomized controlled trial of 200 patients comparing drug A versus placebo for hypertension over 12 weeks. Drug A reduced systolic blood pressure by 12 mmHg (95% CI 8-16, p<0.001). Adverse events were comparable between groups with no serious events attributed to drug A.
output_examples.output:
Highlights
- Drug A reduced systolic blood pressure by 12 mmHg versus placebo at 12 weeks
- Treatment was well-tolerated with adverse events comparable to placebo
- First randomized evidence supporting drug A as first-line monotherapy

output_examples.input: A meta-analysis of 15 randomized controlled trials on cognitive behavioral therapy for insomnia including 1,200 adult patients. CBT-I improved sleep onset latency by 18 minutes versus control. Treatment effects persisted at 6-month follow-up across all subgroups.
output_examples.output:
Highlights
- Meta-analysis of 15 RCTs including 1,200 patients with chronic insomnia
- CBT-I improved sleep onset latency by 18 minutes versus control conditions
- Treatment effects persisted at 6-month follow-up across all subgroups

output_examples.input: A single paragraph with only background information about diabetes prevalence and no specific study methods or results.
output_examples.output:
Cannot generate submission-ready Highlights yet.
Reason: insufficient source
Detected type: Unknown
Minimum additional input needed:
- Study type or research design
- Main findings or results
- Methods or evidence base description

best_practices: Provide complete manuscript text or a structured abstract for the most accurate and comprehensive highlights
Specify the article type and target journal if known, as this enables more precisely tailored output
Review generated highlights against the source material to verify factual accuracy before journal submission
anti_patterns: Do not ask the skill to fabricate results, effect sizes, or novelty claims that are not explicitly present in the source text
Do not provide only a paper title and expect complete highlights; sufficient source text describing methods and findings is required
Do not use this skill for editorials, commentaries, or perspective pieces that lack structured research findings

faq.question: What types of manuscripts can this skill process?
faq.answer: It supports original research, meta-analyses, reviews, case reports, bioinformatics studies, bibliometric analyses, and technical notes. Editorials and commentaries are not suitable.

faq.question: What file formats are supported for text extraction?
faq.answer: The skill supports PDF, DOCX, and TXT files through a Python extraction script. Binary .doc files must be converted to .docx or .pdf format first.

faq.question: How many highlight bullets does the skill generate?
faq.answer: It generates exactly 3 to 5 bullets per output. It will not produce fewer than 3 or more than 5, and it will not pad to 5 if the source only supports 3 safe claims.

faq.question: What is the maximum length per highlight bullet?
faq.answer: Each English bullet is limited to 85 characters. Chinese bullets are limited to 40 characters when Chinese output is explicitly requested.

faq.question: Can the skill generate highlights in languages other than English?
faq.answer: It generates English highlights by default. Chinese output is available only when the user explicitly requests it. No other languages are supported.

faq.question: What happens if the source text is too short or unclear?
faq.answer: The skill will refuse to generate highlights and instead provide a structured explanation of what additional information is needed, including the detected or unknown article type.
